name: Domain Vulnerability Scanner
description: TLS grade, DNSSEC validation, HTTP security headers, subdomain exposure, open ports, threat intelligence for a target domain. The platform's flagship free-conversion entry point.

frontend.evidence: runScan('domain') -> executeScan('domain', payload), part of the 'Choose Your Scan Module' tab grid on the homepage #scanner section. Also the hero 'Scan Free' quick-scan CTA.

navigation.evidence: Primary nav '⚡ Free Scan' link + hero section CTA both route to #scanner, which defaults to the Domain tab.

verification.evidence: Free tier (5/day IP-based) has no tier dependency, unaffected by the 2026-07-10 auth-header fix. Real headless-Chromium Playwright session (locally served build, mocked /api/scan/domain response) confirms executeScan() now attaches Authorization: Bearer <cdb_token> when a JWT is present — same fix as CAP-SCAN-002/003/004/005/006/007/008. Not verified against live production (no deploy access this session); mocked-response Playwright is not equivalent to a live production click-through, hence customer_journey_complete stays false despite the dynamic_browser method (same convention as CAP-IDN-001).

notes: Free-accessible by design (rate-limited by IP, not tier) — subscription_gated correctly false. The 2026-07-10 fix (executeScan() now sends the paying customer's JWT) matters here for STARTER+ customers who get higher daily limits than the anonymous IP-based free tier; before the fix, a logged-in paying customer's domain scans were rate-limited identically to an anonymous visitor.

name: AI Security Scanner (OWASP LLM Top 10)
description: Prompt injection, excessive agency, output handling and other OWASP LLM Top 10 checks against an AI/LLM deployment. Free preview, full findings on paid tiers.

frontend.evidence: runScan('ai') -> executeScan('ai', payload), 'AI Security' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

verification.evidence: Free preview + full-findings-on-paid pattern confirmed via pricing feature matrix ('AI Security (OWASP LLM Top 10): Free=Preview, Starter=full'); handler read directly for module='ai' request shape. Covered by the shared executeScan() auth-header fix (2026-07-10) — before that fix a paying customer's AI scan would have been silently truncated to the free preview regardless of tier, since resolveAuthV5 could never see their JWT.

notes: Soft-gated (preview truncation), not a hard 403 — same fix-impact class as CAP-SCAN-001, not CAP-SCAN-003/004/005 (which hard-block free tier entirely).

name: Red Team Simulation
description: 8 MITRE ATT&CK kill-chain scenarios (spear phishing, password spray, lateral movement, DNS tunneling, cloud privilege escalation, ransomware simulation) run agentlessly. STARTER tier and above only.

frontend.evidence: runScan('redteam') -> executeScan('redteam', payload), 'Red Team' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

verification.evidence: Pricing feature matrix: 'Red Team Simulation: Free=—, Starter=✓'. Gated via the STARTER monthly-quota check inside runSyncPipeline (workers/src/index.js:946) rather than a hard per-request tier block inside the handler itself — confirmed by direct read of runSyncPipeline. Directly affected by the 2026-07-10 executeScan() auth-header fix: before the fix, a STARTER+ customer's redteam scan request always resolved to the anonymous FREE identity (no Authorization header sent), so the tier-based quota path never even engaged correctly for them.

notes: Unlike CAP-SCAN-006/007/008, this module's tier check lives in the shared sync pipeline rather than the handler body — worth confirming during any future refactor of runSyncPipeline that this gate isn't accidentally dropped.

name: Identity & Zero Trust Scanner
description: NIST SP 800-207 aligned Zero Trust maturity assessment: MFA posture, JIT access, Conditional Access, RBAC gaps, privileged account exposure. STARTER tier and above.

frontend.evidence: runScan('identity') -> executeScan('identity', payload), 'Identity' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

verification.evidence: Same gating pattern and same 2026-07-10 auth-header fix dependency as CAP-SCAN-003 (runSyncPipeline STARTER quota check, not an in-handler tier block).

name: Compliance Report Generator
description: ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR/DPDP, PCI-DSS control gap analysis. STARTER tier and above.

frontend.evidence: runScan('compliance') -> executeScan('compliance', payload), 'Compliance' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

verification.evidence: Same gating pattern and same 2026-07-10 auth-header fix dependency as CAP-SCAN-003/004.

name: Cloud Security Posture Scanner
description: CSPM across AWS/GCP/Azure — IAM, storage permissions, encryption, logging, network ACLs. PRO tier and above.

frontend.evidence: runScan('cloudsec') -> executeScan('cloudsec', payload), 'Cloud Security' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

test_coverage.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-12: workers/test/cloudSecurityScanner.test.mjs (12 tests) — the real 27-control CIS-aligned scoring engine (0%/F/HIGH_RISK when nothing implemented, 100%/A/SECURE when everything is, real specific remediation text per control, live-shaped public-S3-bucket and DNS-cloud-provider detection, graceful no-domain handling) plus the full tier gate on handleCloudSecurityScan (FREE/STARTER/anonymous rejected with 403+upgrade_url, PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP admitted, isAdmin bypass admitted) — previously zero test coverage of any kind for this handler.

priority_reason: no tests at all, plus the highest-severity instance of the pre-2026-07-10 auth-header bug: this handler hard-403s any request whose authCtx.tier isn't PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP (workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:335). Before the fix, every paying PRO+ customer attempting this scan was flatly rejected with 'PRO plan or above required' — not degraded, completely blocked — since executeScan() never sent their JWT and resolveAuthV5 always fell through to the anonymous FREE identity.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:334-354 read directly. Route dispatch (workers/src/index.js:5793-5795) calls resolveAuthV5(request, env) directly rather than through runSyncPipeline/SYNC_ROUTES — a materially different auth-resolution path from CAP-SCAN-001..005, but resolveAuthV5 itself is the same function, so the 2026-07-10 fix (executeScan() now attaches the JWT) applies here too since this module is one of the 8 dispatched through executeScan(). FIXED 2026-07-12: workers/src/services/cloudSecurityEngine.js read in full (real 27-control CIS Cloud Security Benchmark v1.5 / CSA CCM v4 scoring, real public-bucket + DNS-CNAME exposure checks, no fabrication) before writing tests against its real shape. Full suite green: 257 files / 2645 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-12: the zero test coverage this entry flagged as the most pressing residual gap is closed — see test_coverage.evidence. operational_status was already PILOT ONLY (not NOT READY) since the underlying scan engine and tier gate were both already correct; only the lack of a regression test was the actual risk (a silent tier-gate regression would not have been caught before this fix).

name: Dark Web Exposure Scanner
description: Dark web markets, breach databases, paste sites, threat forums exposure check. PRO tier and above.

frontend.evidence: runScan('darkscan') -> executeScan('darkscan', payload), 'Dark Web' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/appsecDarkwebScanners.test.mjs imports handleDarkWebScan. FIXED 2026-07-12: the tier-gate coverage this entry's own notes flagged as unconfirmed was checked directly and found genuinely partial — FREE-tier rejection was tested for the sibling appsec handler but not darkscan, and STARTER-tier rejection plus the isAdmin bypass weren't tested for either handler. Added: FREE rejected (403+upgrade_url), STARTER rejected, ENTERPRISE/MSSP admitted, isAdmin bypass admitted, anonymous caller rejected — all for handleDarkWebScan specifically, not just inferred from its sibling.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:411-417 read directly — same hard-403-if-not-PRO+ pattern as CAP-SCAN-006, same pre-2026-07-10 impact (complete lockout of paying PRO+ customers, not degradation), same fix now applies (executeScan() sends the JWT). FIXED 2026-07-12: workers/test/appsecDarkwebScanners.test.mjs read in full before extending it, to add real coverage rather than assuming the existing tests already implied the missing cases. Full suite green: 257 files / 2645 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-12: the prior note's open question — does the existing test file actually cover the PRO+ tier gate specifically — is answered and closed. It covered PRO-admitted and FREE-rejected for appsec, and MSSP-admitted for darkweb, but not FREE-rejected for darkweb, not STARTER for either, and not the isAdmin bypass for either. All gaps closed in the same file.

name: AppSec / DAST Scanner
description: OWASP Top 10 web app testing — injection, broken auth, XSS, IDOR, rate limiting. PRO tier and above.

frontend.evidence: runScan('appsec') -> executeScan('appsec', payload), 'AppSec' tab in the scan module grid.

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the same #scanner tab grid as CAP-SCAN-001.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/appsecDarkwebScanners.test.mjs imports handleAppSecScan. FIXED 2026-07-12: extended alongside CAP-SCAN-007's fix in the same file — added STARTER-tier rejection, MSSP admission, isAdmin bypass admission, and anonymous-caller rejection for handleAppSecScan specifically (FREE-rejected and PRO-admitted were already covered).

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/serviceHandlers.js:386-392 read directly — same hard-403-if-not-PRO+ pattern and same 2026-07-10 fix dependency as CAP-SCAN-006/007. Full suite green: 257 files / 2645 tests. Registry validator: 0 failures, 0 warnings.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-12: tier-gate test coverage rounded out to match CAP-SCAN-007's fix (same file, same pass) — STARTER-rejected, MSSP-admitted, isAdmin-bypass-admitted, anonymous-rejected now all explicitly tested for this handler too.

name: MCP Security Scanner
description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool-permission, auth-token, and prompt-injection surface audit. Marketed as free/unrestricted ("World's First" scanner positioning).

frontend.evidence: runMCPScan() -> POST /api/mcp-security/scan, its own dedicated tab ('🔌 MCP Security') plus a homepage CTA ('🔌 Scan MCP Server Free →').

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the #scanner tab grid and a dedicated homepage CTA.

test_coverage.evidence: grep for handleMCPSecurityScan across workers/test/**/*.mjs returns 0 matches (verified 2026-07-09).

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/mcpSecurityScanner.js:265-334 read in full — confirmed no tier/auth gate of any kind exists in this handler (intentional: free lead-magnet feature, not a gap). Real headless-Chromium Playwright session (locally served build, mocked /api/mcp-security/scan response) confirms runMCPScan() now attaches Authorization: Bearer <cdb_token> when present (2026-07-10 fix) — has no functional effect here since the handler doesn't read authCtx.tier, but keeps this call site consistent with the other 9 modules for any future gating. Not verified against live production; customer_journey_complete stays false per the same convention as CAP-SCAN-001 (mocked-response Playwright ≠ live production).

notes: Zero tier gating is confirmed intentional (marketing positioning), not a P5 subscription-gating gap. Zero test coverage is a real, separate gap — this handler also writes to D1 (mcp_security_scans, funnel_events) with no test verifying those writes.

name: Vibe Code Security Scanner
description: Static analysis of AI-generated code — hardcoded secrets, SQLi patterns, SSRF vectors, auth gaps, AI-generation likelihood. Free preview with locked findings; full detail on paid tiers via applyTierGate().

frontend.evidence: runVibeCodeScan() -> POST /api/vibe-code/scan, its own dedicated tab ('🤖 Vibe Code') plus a homepage CTA ('🤖 Scan Vibe Code Free →').

navigation.evidence: Reachable via the #scanner tab grid and a dedicated homepage CTA.

test_coverage.evidence: grep for handleVibeCodeScan across workers/test/**/*.mjs returns 0 matches (verified 2026-07-09) — notable given this is the largest scan module by dedicated source (4 files: scanner, engine, rules, util).

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/vibe-code/vibeCodeScanner.js:133-169 read in full — plan resolved via tierFromAuth(authCtx) then applyTierGate() truncates findings for non-paid tiers rather than a hard block (same soft-gate class as CAP-SCAN-002). Real headless-Chromium Playwright session (locally served build, mocked /api/vibe-code/scan response) confirms runVibeCodeScan() now attaches Authorization: Bearer <cdb_token> when present (2026-07-10 fix) — before the fix, a paying customer's vibe-code scans were always truncated to the free-preview tier since authCtx.tier could never resolve above FREE. Not verified against live production; customer_journey_complete stays false per the same convention as CAP-SCAN-001/009.

notes: Largest untested surface of the 10 modules (4 dedicated files, 0 tests) — recommend this as the top test-coverage priority for a future wave, ahead of CAP-SCAN-006/009 which are single-file.
